summary: Educational assistance for spouses and dependents of disabled veterans in Michigan.

benefit_name: Michigan Veteran Survivor and Dependent Benefits
summary: Michigan offers state-level benefits for surviving spouses, dependents, and family members of veterans. Programs may include education tuition waivers or scholarships, survivor pension supplements, priority access to state veteran services, and continued eligibility for select state programs. Specific programs and eligibility requirements vary.
eligibility: Surviving spouses, unremarried widows/widowers, and dependent children of eligible Michigan veterans. Some programs require the veteran to have had a service-connected disability or to have died in service. Residency in Michigan is typically required. Contact the Michigan veterans agency for current program availability.

benefit_name: Michigan Enhanced Benefits for Service-Connected Disabled Veterans
summary: Michigan provides a package of enhanced state benefits for veterans with a service-connected disability rating. Benefits may include increased property tax exemptions at higher rating tiers, priority access to state veteran services, healthcare facility preference, adaptive equipment assistance, and waived fees across multiple state programs. Specific benefits and thresholds vary by rating level.
eligibility: Veterans with any service-connected disability rating who are residents of Michigan. Many programs require a minimum 10% rating; higher-value benefits typically require 50% or 100% ratings. Contact the Michigan veterans agency for current eligibility thresholds.

summary: Property tax exemption for disabled veterans with service-connected disabilities in Michigan.
eligibility: Veterans with 10% or higher service-connected disability rating.

benefit_name: North Carolina Veteran Housing Assistance and HUD-VASH Referrals
summary: North Carolina does not operate a state-funded veteran homeownership loan program. The NC Division of Military and Veterans Affairs connects veterans in housing need to federal HUD-VASH (Veterans Affairs Supportive Housing) vouchers for rental assistance, SSVF emergency housing funds for veterans at risk of homelessness, and county-level housing navigators through the NC Veterans Service Officer network.
eligibility: North Carolina veterans experiencing homelessness or housing instability. HUD-VASH requires VA healthcare enrollment. SSVF programs serve veterans and families below 50% Area Median Income. Contact your county Veterans Service Officer for referrals.
administering_agency: NC Division of Military and Veterans Affairs

summary: Educational assistance for spouses and dependents of disabled veterans in Nevada.
eligibility: Spouses and dependents of veterans with 100% service-connected disability.

benefit_name: Nevada Veteran Survivor and Dependent Benefits
summary: Nevada offers state-level benefits for surviving spouses, dependents, and family members of veterans. Programs may include education tuition waivers or scholarships, survivor pension supplements, priority access to state veteran services, and continued eligibility for select state programs. Specific programs and eligibility requirements vary.
eligibility: Surviving spouses, unremarried widows/widowers, and dependent children of eligible Nevada veterans. Some programs require the veteran to have had a service-connected disability or to have died in service. Residency in Nevada is typically required. Contact the Nevada veterans agency for current program availability.

benefit_name: Nevada Enhanced Benefits for Service-Connected Disabled Veterans
summary: Nevada provides a package of enhanced state benefits for veterans with a service-connected disability rating. Benefits may include increased property tax exemptions at higher rating tiers, priority access to state veteran services, healthcare facility preference, adaptive equipment assistance, and waived fees across multiple state programs. Specific benefits and thresholds vary by rating level.
eligibility: Veterans with any service-connected disability rating who are residents of Nevada. Many programs require a minimum 10% rating; higher-value benefits typically require 50% or 100% ratings. Contact the Nevada veterans agency for current eligibility thresholds.

summary: Property tax exemption for disabled veterans with service-connected disabilities in Nevada.
eligibility: Veterans with 10% or higher service-connected disability rating.

summary: Housing assistance and loan programs for eligible Nevada veterans.
eligibility: Honorably discharged veterans who are state residents.

summary: Discounted or free hunting and fishing licenses for disabled veterans in Nevada.
eligibility: Disabled veterans with service-connected disabilities.

summary: In-state tuition rates for veterans at Nevada public universities and colleges.
eligibility: Honorably discharged veterans who are state residents.

summary: State-operated long-term care facility for eligible Nevada veterans.
eligibility: Nevada resident veterans with honorable discharge.

summary: Fee waivers for professional license applications for Nevada veterans.
eligibility: Honorably discharged veterans.

summary: Business certification and support for service-disabled veteran entrepreneurs in Nevada.
eligibility: Veterans with 10% or higher service-connected disability rating.

summary: Hiring preference for state government positions for Nevada veterans.
eligibility: Honorably discharged veterans.

eligibility: Honorably discharged veterans and eligible family members.

eligibility: Honorably discharged veterans.

summary: The State of New York Mortgage Agency (SONYMA) Homes for Veterans Program offers veterans fixed-rate mortgage loans at interest rates 0.40% below standard SONYMA rates, plus down payment assistance up to $15,000 (3% of purchase price). First-time homebuyer status is not required. Loans are processed through participating SONYMA-approved lenders.
eligibility: Honorably or other-than-dishonorably discharged New York veterans. No first-time homebuyer requirement. Income and purchase price limits apply by county. Veterans must work with a SONYMA-participating lender to apply.
administering_agency: State of New York Mortgage Agency (SONYMA)
